**Additional Information**
[Additional information that was discussed]
He's been dealing with knee issues for a while, and it's time for surgery. He's got a total knee replacement scheduled for March twenty-eight with Dr. Volkov. He's been dealing with knee pain for a while and has had knee issues for a few years. Cortisone shots stopped working, and PT didn't help much. Dr. Volkov's done some X-rays, and they show bone-on-bone arthritis. He's been dealing with that for a while.

His diabetes is controlled with metformin, and he's on losartan. He's also on atorvastatin. He's been using a CPAP machine for a few years. He's been smoking for about fifteen years and quit eight years ago. He's got obstructive sleep apnea and uses a CPAP machine. He's been on it for four years. He's been doing well with his diet.

For labs, I ordered a CBC, basic metabolic panel, hemoglobin A1C, and coagulation studies. He's going to the lab today after the visit. His last A1C was three months ago, and I want to see where it is now, since elevated blood sugar increases infection risk perioperatively. He's been doing well with his diet lately.

I also performed an EKG today as requested by Dr. Volkov's office. The EKG showed normal sinus rhythm, no ST changes, no conduction delays. I'll send a copy to Dr. Volkov's office and the anesthesia team along with my clearance note.

I counseled him on the recovery timeline — first two weeks are the toughest, PT starts essentially the day after surgery, most patients feel significantly better by three months, and improvement continues up to a year. Outcomes for total knee replacement are generally very good.

**Conclusion**
Tom is cleared for surgery. I'll send the clearance note to Dr. Volkov's office today. He has his instructions — stop aspirin and fish oil on March twenty-first, hold metformin and losartan the morning of surgery, bring the CPAP, and call us if anything changes before the twenty-eight, including any illness or fever.
